summary.narrative: Mayor Mahan has made incremental progress on public safety and homelessness, with resident perception of safety showing modest improvement. His tough-on-crime stance led him to champion Proposition 36, which passed statewide. However, some infrastructure and housing promises remain in progress or stalled amid budget constraints.

promises.text: Increase public safety and make San Jose residents feel safer
promises.evidence: Resident surveys show 65% view San Jose as safe in Q3 FY2024-25, up from 63% the prior year, indicating modest improvement in perceived safety.

promises.text: Support tougher penalties for repeat drug and theft offenses
promises.evidence: Mahan was a leading supporter of Proposition 36, which passed in November 2024, implementing tougher penalties for repeat drug and theft crimes.

promises.text: Reduce homelessness through increased shelter capacity and enforcement
promises.evidence: The city has expanded interim housing options but homelessness remains a significant challenge with encampments persisting throughout the city.

promises.text: Build 1,000 interim housing units for homeless residents
promises.evidence: Progress has been made on expanding interim housing capacity but the full 1,000-unit goal has not yet been completed.

promises.text: Improve city infrastructure including pothole repairs and street maintenance
promises.evidence: Budget constraints have limited the pace of infrastructure improvements, with residents continuing to report delays in pothole repairs.

promises.text: Hold city council members accountable for misconduct
promises.evidence: In October 2024, Mahan publicly called for Councilmember Omar Torres to resign following child sexual misconduct allegations; Torres subsequently resigned and was arrested.

promises.text: Expand police staffing to improve emergency response times
promises.evidence: The city has worked to recruit additional officers but remains below targeted staffing levels due to competitive hiring challenges.

promises.text: Streamline permitting to accelerate housing construction
promises.evidence: Despite stated goals, permitting timelines have not significantly improved and developers continue to report bureaucratic delays.
